Reinforcement Learning Overview: Reinforcement learning (RL) is a framework for sequential decision-making where an agent learns to maximize cumulative reward by interacting with an environment. Model-free methods like Q-learning and policy gradient algorithms (REINFORCE, PPO, SAC) learn directly from environment interactions, while model-based methods learn a world model to plan or generate synthetic experience. Deep RL, combining deep neural networks with RL algorithms, has achieved superhuman performance on Atari games (DQN), Go and Chess (AlphaZero), and robotic manipulation tasks. The key challenges of sample efficiency, exploration, reward shaping, and sim-to-real transfer remain active research areas.
